_meta.description: D6 fixtures for hermes / multimodal. The demo dispatches a bundled sample (image or PDF) plus a canned prompt via agent.addMessage + copilotkit.runAgent. The hermes AG-UI adapter forwards image content parts to the vision model natively (translate._content_to_parts); PDF 'document' parts carry no text so only the prompt text survives — either way aimock matches on the prompt substring and returns the canned reply. Keyed on the verbatim auto-prompts; distinct substrings ('demo image' vs 'demo pdf') so no turnIndex gate is needed. The image reply contains 'image' and the PDF reply contains 'document' to satisfy the D5 modality assertions.

fixtures._comment: multimodal — sample image turn. The probe clicks 'Try with sample image', which auto-sends prompt 'can you tell me what is in this demo image I just attached'. Assertion checks the assistant transcript contains 'image'.

response.content: The image attachment shows a small abstract test pattern used by the multimodal demo to validate the image-upload pipeline. Successful render of this response confirms the binary image attachment round-tripped through the runtime to the vision-capable model.

fixtures._comment: multimodal — sample PDF turn. The probe clicks 'Try with sample PDF', which auto-sends prompt 'can you tell me what is in this demo pdf I just attached'. Assertion checks the assistant transcript contains 'document'.

response.content: The PDF document contains a single test page used by the multimodal demo. Receiving this response confirms the document attachment reached the runtime; hermes forwards the accompanying prompt text to the model (there is no PDF-text-flatten step, so the document body itself is not extracted).
